ip_outcome_note: Confirmed Kanto-form Meowth: cream-colored bipedal cat with gold forehead koban coin matches sm3-101 record and Bulbapedia. National Pokedex no. 52, Normal-type.

trivia_bullets.text: Kanto Meowth's forehead coin is an Edo-period koban; the entire design is rooted in maneki-neko. Japanese name Nyarth references a proverb about a cat unknowingly having money ('casting pearls before swine' equivalent), underscoring coin obsession without comprehension.

Pay Day (Generation I signature move) has Meowth hurl koban at opponents; coins are collectible after battle. The move literalizes the forehead-coin design into a combat function. This card's oracle carries Fake Out, not Pay Day, but the coin art is the same forehead koban.
"It lies around all day, becoming active near dusk. At night, it wanders the city in search of loose change." Urban coin-scavenging reinforces the maneki-neko/koban obsession origin.

Kanto-form vs. Galarian-form contrast: this print (Normal-type, cream, golden coin, evolves into Persian) pairs with corpus card Galarian Meowth (Rebel Clash 126/192, Steel-type, iron coin, evolves into Perrserker). Same National Pokedex no. 52, divergent in every other respect.
